differential_diagnoses: Drug-induced hepatotoxicity with secondary cardiac manifestations
next_steps: Lyme disease serology (IgM and IgG) and Western blot
Echocardiogram to assess cardiac structure and function
Discontinuation of all herbal supplements and non-essential medications
Inflammatory markers (ESR, CRP) and autoantibody panel
Consider empiric ceftriaxone if clinical suspicion for Lyme remains high while awaiting serology

differential_diagnoses: Q fever (Coxiella burnetii) endocarditis or myocarditis
next_steps: Admit to cardiac telemetry for continuous rhythm monitoring
Cardiac MRI with gadolinium to characterize myocardial tissue (looking for late gadolinium enhancement)
Lyme disease serology (IgM and IgG) and western blot
Serologic testing for Coxiella burnetii (Q fever) and Francisella tularensis
Transthoracic echocardiogram to evaluate for valvular vegetation or structural wall motion abnormalities
Discontinue all herbal and non-essential supplements
Empiric intravenous ceftriaxone for potential Lyme carditis if conduction block persists or worsens
Consultation with cardiology and potentially electrophysiology
